title: Core Questions to Answer for Essays — scholarship prompt master
html:
TL;DR

  • Dec 23, 2020 master file collecting the actual essay prompts from JCEF, OSU, FORD Scholarship, and Renaissance (with Al Forthan header on the next page) — the organizing doc behind all of Yahir's senior-year scholarship writing.
  • Self-coined frames at the top: "5 snapshots — situation, insight afterwards" (JCEF strategy), "Scream character" (FORD framing — likely a personal-voice device), "New birth" (Renaissance framing).
  • Recurring prompt families: family/community service, personal accomplishment, significant change, values/regret incident, what-you're-known-for, place-of-origin, lose-track-of-time topic, 10-year-future-life.
  • Renaissance Essay #1 (1,000 words): the obstacles-as-fundamental-to-success prompt — directly precursor to Yahir's May resubmission ask to Diana Hoff.
  • Companion to the national scholarships catalog from 19 days earlier; together they're the planning scaffolding for the Grants Pass funding push that ultimately landed Ford + Renaissance.

Notes

This is metadata about the essays, not the essays themselves — so it lives outside the standard "skip scholarship essays" rule. The frames Yahir invented to remember each org's vibe ("Scream character," "New birth," "5 snapshots") show him treating each scholarship as a different rhetorical genre rather than recycling one essay. That's craft.

The duplicated prompt across JCEF / OSU / FORD ("What have you done for your family or community...") explains why Yahir's debt of gratitude frame solidified during this season — he was being asked the same question by four different funders, in slightly different word counts. The answer became canonical.

Connections

  • 2020 12 04 national scholarships december 2020 — companion catalog
  • college admissions — the season
  • jcef · ford family foundation · renaissance scholarship · al forthan · osu — the recipients
  • debt of gratitude — the rhetorical frame these prompts forced Yahir to crystallize
  • 2021 04 13 diana hoff renaissance email — the Renaissance follow-through 4 months later
